news.t: Why high-yield bond ETFs may deliver ‘surprise' outperformance in fixed income in 2024
Heightened rate volatility points to these opportunities in stocks, bonds for ETF investors, says State Street's Michael Arone

overview.description: The State Street SPDR Portfolio Intermediate Term Corporate Bond ETF (SPIB) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in investment grade fixed income. The fund tracks a market-value-weighted index of investment-grade, fixed-rate taxable US corporate bonds with a maturity of at least one year, but no more than 10 years. SPIB was launched on Feb 10, 2009 and is issued by State Street.
